Shared KV: tying keys and values in multi-head attention

Standard multi-head attention projects the input into separate queries, keys, and values via \(W_Q\), \(W_K\), \(W_V\). Shared KV eliminates the separate key projection: a single matrix \(W_{KV}\) produces a representation that is used as both the value and (after RoPE) the key, halving the KV cache at inference. The modification was tested at 270M and 1B parameter scales on C4 on a v4-32 TPU pod slice.

At 270M with matched learning rate (\(3 \times 10^{-4}\)), shared KV (EMA loss 2.88) is 0.025 nats behind the standard baseline (2.85). At 1B, shared KV (2.51) is 0.018 nats behind the baseline (2.49). Both gaps are small relative to the 50% KV cache reduction.

Architecture

In a standard transformer layer, the KV projection is:

\[k, v = x W_{KV} \quad \text{where } W_{KV} \in \mathbb{R}^{2 \times d_\text{model} \times n_{kv} \times d_\text{head}}\]

The shared KV variant replaces this with:

\[v = x W_{KV} \quad \text{where } W_{KV} \in \mathbb{R}^{d_\text{model} \times n_{kv} \times d_\text{head}}\]

\[k = \text{RoPE}(v)\]

The key is derived from the value by applying rotary position embeddings. At inference, the KV cache stores only one tensor per layer instead of two, halving KV cache memory.

Implementation: shared kv implementation.

Code 
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.io as pio

pio.renderers.default = "plotly_mimetype+notebook_connected"

# 270M experiments
SHARED_KV_270M_DIR = Path("../clearml_experiments/c4_a100x8x4_270m/shared_kv")
BASELINE_270M_DIR = Path("../clearml_experiments/c4_a100x8x4_270m/mup/270m_3e_4_main_2fbbd3a9216d4e9c963b48b70fcebd89")

# 1B experiments
SHARED_KV_1B_DIR = Path("../clearml_experiments/c4_a100x8x4_1b/shared_kv")
BASELINE_1B_DIR = Path("../clearml_experiments/c4_a100x8x4_1b/main")

TRACKED_FIELDS = [
    ("learning_rate", "Learning_Rate"),
    ("d_model", "d_model"),
    ("n_kv", "n_kv"),
    ("d_head", "d_head"),
    ("layers", "Layers"),
    ("d_ff", "d_ff"),
]

SHARED_DEFAULTS_270M = {
    "d_model": 1024,
    "d_ff": 8192,
    "n_kv": 16,
    "d_head": 128,
    "layers": 8,
    "learning_rate": 3e-4,
}

SHARED_DEFAULTS_1B = {
    "d_model": 2048,
    "d_ff": 16384,
    "n_kv": 16,
    "d_head": 128,
    "layers": 8,
    "learning_rate": 3e-4,
}


def calculate_ema(data, smoothing=0.97):
    ema = [data[0]]
    for value in data[1:]:
        ema.append(ema[-1] * smoothing + value * (1 - smoothing))
    return ema


def _read_csv(path):
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    return df["step"].tolist(), df["value"].tolist()


def _parse_config(metadata):
    hp = metadata.get("hyperparameters", {})
    config = {}
    for key, val in hp.items():
        if not key.startswith("Hydra/"):
            continue
        suffix = key.removeprefix("Hydra/")
        if suffix.startswith("model."):
            config[suffix.removeprefix("model.")] = val
        elif suffix.startswith("training."):
            config[suffix.removeprefix("training.")] = val
    return config


def _load_experiment(exp_dir, metrics_data, config_data, kind="shared_kv", defaults=None):
    metadata = json.loads((exp_dir / "metadata.json").read_text())
    if metadata.get("status") == "failed":
        return
    task_id = metadata["experiment_id"]
    config_data[task_id] = _parse_config(metadata)
    loss_csv = _read_csv(exp_dir / "metrics" / "loss_loss.csv")
    if loss_csv is None:
        return
    steps, loss = loss_csv
    lr_csv = _read_csv(exp_dir / "metrics" / "learning_rate_learning_rate.csv")
    peak_lr = max(v for v in lr_csv[1]) if lr_csv else None
    if peak_lr is not None and "learning_rate" not in config_data[task_id]:
        config_data[task_id]["learning_rate"] = peak_lr
    name = metadata["experiment_name"]
    metrics_data[task_id] = {
        "name": name,
        "steps": steps,
        "loss": loss,
        "final_loss": calculate_ema(loss)[-1],
        "kind": kind,
        "peak_lr": peak_lr,
    }


def get_metrics_data(experiment_dirs, baseline_dirs, exp_defaults=None, base_defaults=None):
    metrics_data, config_data = {}, {}
    for exp_dir in experiment_dirs:
        for run_dir in sorted(exp_dir.iterdir()):
            if run_dir.is_dir():
                _load_experiment(run_dir, metrics_data, config_data, kind="shared_kv", defaults=exp_defaults)
    for base_dir in baseline_dirs:
        for run_dir in sorted(base_dir.iterdir()):
            if run_dir.is_dir():
                _load_experiment(run_dir, metrics_data, config_data, kind="baseline", defaults=base_defaults)
    return metrics_data, config_data


def filter_metrics(metrics_data, kinds=None):
    out = {}
    for guid, d in metrics_data.items():
        if kinds is not None and d["kind"] not in kinds:
            continue
        out[guid] = d
    return out


def get_top_k_experiments(loss_data, k=None, ema_smoothing=0.97):
    sorted_exps = sorted(loss_data.items(), key=lambda x: calculate_ema(x[1]["loss"], smoothing=ema_smoothing)[-1])
    return dict(sorted_exps[:k] if k else sorted_exps)


def plot_loss_data(loss_data, ema_smoothing=0.97, top_k=None, opacity=0.25, title=None):
    if top_k:
        loss_data = get_top_k_experiments(loss_data, k=top_k, ema_smoothing=ema_smoothing)
    palette = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd", "#8c564b",
               "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f", "#bcbd22", "#17becf"]
    fig = go.Figure()
    for i, (_, data) in enumerate(loss_data.items()):
        steps, loss = data["steps"], data["loss"]
        ema = calculate_ema(loss, smoothing=ema_smoothing)
        color = palette[i % len(palette)]
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=steps, y=loss, mode="lines", name=data["name"],
            legendgroup=data["name"], showlegend=False,
            line=dict(color=color, width=1), opacity=opacity, hoverinfo="skip",
        ))
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=steps, y=ema, mode="lines", name=data["name"],
            legendgroup=data["name"],
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            hovertemplate="%{fullData.name}<br>step=%{x}<br>loss=%{y:.4f}<extra></extra>",
        ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title=title,
        xaxis_title="Steps", yaxis_title="Loss",
        height=600, hovermode="closest",
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.15, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
        margin=dict(l=60, r=20, t=40, b=80),
    )
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0.1)")
    fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0.1)")
    fig.show()


def get_eval_metrics_table(metrics_data, config_data, defaults=None, top_k=None):
    if defaults is None:
        defaults = {}
    rows = []
    for guid, d in metrics_data.items():
        if d["final_loss"] is None:
            continue
        fields = {}
        cfg = config_data[guid]
        for config_key, display_key in TRACKED_FIELDS:
            val = cfg.get(config_key)
            if val is None:
                val = defaults.get(config_key)
            if val is None:
                continue
            if config_key == "learning_rate":
                fields[display_key] = f"{float(val):.1e}"
            else:
                fields[display_key] = int(val) if isinstance(val, (int, float)) else val
        name = d["name"]
        rows.append({
            "Name": name,
            "Kind": d["kind"],
            "Final Loss (EMA)": round(float(d["final_loss"]), 4),
            "Steps": max(d["steps"]),
            **fields,
        })
    df = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("Final Loss (EMA)").reset_index(drop=True)
    if top_k:
        df = df.head(top_k)
    return df.style.format(precision=4).set_properties(**{"text-align": "left"})


# Load all data
metrics_270m, config_270m = get_metrics_data(
    [SHARED_KV_270M_DIR], [],
    exp_defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_270M, base_defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_270M,
)
_load_experiment(BASELINE_270M_DIR, metrics_270m, config_270m, kind="baseline", defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_270M)
metrics_1b, config_1b = get_metrics_data(
    [SHARED_KV_1B_DIR], [BASELINE_1B_DIR],
    exp_defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_1B, base_defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_1B,
)

270M: shared KV vs baselines

One shared KV run and one baseline were trained at 270M on C4 for 23,000 steps at lr \(3 \times 10^{-4}\). Four shared KV seed variants produced identical loss curves (flat-tokens loader is deterministic); only one is shown.

Code 
completed_270m = filter_metrics(metrics_270m, kinds=("shared_kv", "baseline"))

shared_kv_ids = [gid for gid, d in completed_270m.items() if d["kind"] == "shared_kv"]
baseline_ids = [gid for gid, d in completed_270m.items() if d["kind"] == "baseline"]
representative_270m = {shared_kv_ids[0]: completed_270m[shared_kv_ids[0]]}
representative_270m.update({gid: completed_270m[gid] for gid in baseline_ids})
plot_loss_data(representative_270m, ema_smoothing=0.97, opacity=0.1, title="270M: Shared KV vs Baselines")
Code 
get_eval_metrics_table(representative_270m, config_270m, defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_270M)
  Name Kind Final Loss (EMA) Steps Learning_Rate d_model n_kv d_head Layers d_ff
0 270m_3e-4_main baseline 2.8540 22995 3.0e-04 1024 16 128 8 8192
1 270m_3e-4_shared_kv shared_kv 2.8795 22995 3.0e-04 1024 16 128 8 8192

1B: shared KV vs baselines

One shared KV run and one baseline were trained at 1B on C4 for 92,500 steps. Both use learning rate \(3 \times 10^{-4}\) under standard parametrization.

Code 
completed_1b = filter_metrics(metrics_1b, kinds=("shared_kv", "baseline"))
plot_loss_data(completed_1b, ema_smoothing=0.97, opacity=0.1, title="1B: Shared KV vs Baselines")
Code 
get_eval_metrics_table(completed_1b, config_1b, defaults=SHARED_DEFAULTS_1B)
  Name Kind Final Loss (EMA) Steps Learning_Rate d_model n_kv d_head Layers d_ff
0 c4_a100x8x4_1b_sp baseline 2.4929 92492 3.0e-04 2048 16 128 8 16384
1 c4_a100x8x4_1b_shared_kv shared_kv 2.5112 92492 3.0e-04 2048 16 128 8 16384

Summary

Scale Variant LR Final EMA Loss Steps
270M 270m_3e-4_main (baseline) \(3 \times 10^{-4}\) 2.85 22,995
270M 270m_3e-4_shared_kv \(3 \times 10^{-4}\) 2.88 22,995
1B c4_a100x8x4_1b_sp (baseline) \(3 \times 10^{-4}\) 2.49 92,492
1B c4_a100x8x4_1b_shared_kv \(3 \times 10^{-4}\) 2.51 92,492

At matched learning rate, shared KV is 0.025 nats behind at 270M and 0.018 nats behind at 1B, while using 50% fewer KV projection parameters and halving KV cache memory.

Limitations

  • All baselines use MHA (n_kv=16, n_q_per_kv=1). No comparison against GQA or MQA, which also reduce KV cache.
  • Only one shared KV run exists at each scale.
  • Learning rate (\(3 \times 10^{-4}\)) is the untuned default from c4_a100x8_base.yaml. A tuned LR may shift the gap in either direction.
  • No eval loss, perplexity, or downstream task metrics were recorded. All comparisons use training loss only.
  • No inference latency or KV cache memory measurements were taken.

Next experiments

Shared KV halves KV cache by tying K and V. GQA with n_kv=8 also halves KV cache relative to MHA with n_kv=16. The two strategies achieve the same 2x KV compression through different mechanisms and can be combined. The relevant comparisons at 270M and 1B:

Experiment KV cache (relative to MHA)
MHA (n_kv=16, n_q_per_kv=1) 1x
GQA (n_kv=8, n_q_per_kv=2) 0.5x
Shared KV (n_kv=16) 0.5x
Shared KV + GQA (n_kv=8, n_q_per_kv=2) 0.25x
MQA (n_kv=1, n_q_per_kv=16) 1/16x
